seoTitle: How to Use Webflow Templates to Start a Project Fast
metaDescription: Learn how to browse, clone, and customize a Webflow template so you can launch a professional-looking site without building from scratch.
intro: Starting from a template can save hours of layout work, but only if you know how to customize it without breaking the design system underneath. This guide covers browsing the Webflow marketplace, cloning a layout, and updating colors, copy, and CMS collections. It's for anyone who wants a fast, consistent starting point for a new site.

steps.heading: Browse the template marketplace
steps.body: Open Webflow's official template marketplace and use the filters to find a layout that fits your project type and industry.

steps.heading: Preview and clone the template
steps.body: Use the responsive preview to check how the design looks across devices, then clone the free or purchased template into your account.

steps.heading: Update global styles
steps.body: Open the style guide and change the brand colors, typography, and other global classes so the template reflects your brand.

steps.heading: Replace placeholder content
steps.body: Swap out placeholder images and rewrite the template's text so it matches your actual product, service, or company.

steps.heading: Adjust CMS collections and publish
steps.body: Modify any dynamic collections to fit your content structure, check responsive breakpoints, then publish the site to your domain.

faq.question: Do I need to pay for every Webflow template?
faq.answer: No, Webflow offers a mix of free and premium templates, with premium ones typically offering more advanced layouts and CMS structures.

faq.question: Can I change a template's layout structure completely?
faq.answer: Yes, since it's a normal Webflow project once cloned, you can rearrange sections and elements just like a site built from scratch.

faq.question: Will editing a template break its responsive design?
faq.answer: It can if you're not careful, so it's worth checking each breakpoint after major changes to make sure spacing and alignment still hold up.

faq.question: How do I update the SEO settings on template pages?
faq.answer: Open each page's settings panel and update the meta title and description fields to match your actual content.
